decision_point: In an adult with mild cubital tunnel syndrome (McGowan I: sensory symptoms without motor weakness or muscle wasting), is conservative management or surgical decompression preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with mild cubital tunnel syndrome (McGowan I: sensory symptoms without motor weakness or muscle wasting), is conservative management or surgical decompression preferred?
Options:
  1. Conservative (night extension splinting, activity modification)
  2. Surgical decompression
label: settled
label_rationale: Mild disease is managed non-operatively; motor deficit or atrophy is what indicates surgery.
